chunks.text: An embedding is a numerical representation of text, images, or other data in the form of a vector.
Vector databases store embeddings and allow fast similarity searches using methods such as cosine similarity.
Embeddings capture the semantic meaning of text, enabling similarity searches based on meaning rather than exact keyword matches.
Popular vector database systems are used in recommendation engines, semantic search, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) applications.
Common similarity metrics used in vector databases include cosine similarity, Euclidean distance, and dot product.
Before storing data in a vector database, documents are typically split into smaller chunks and converted into embedding vectors using an embedding model.
